adversarial_review.summary: Final devils-advocate pass cleared P0s and left execution gates centered on shift-intelligence split, alias policy, blast-radius, tests, and rollback artifacts.

notes.checkpoint_purpose: WS1 shared contract foundation landed; ready for Phase 2 internal service bundles.
notes.next_action: Dispatch Phase 2 (WS2 player-financial/loyalty/mtl/table-context + WS3 rating-slip core) in parallel via backend-service-builder.
notes.ws0_summary: Resolved GATE-070.6 by deferring shift-intelligence public DTO shape changes to Phase 1.2 while keeping internal authority routing in Phase 1.1; resolved GATE-070.7 with a no-alias hard rename for totalCashOut; verified direct-coupling inventory, minimum test matrix, and rollback skeleton recorded in EXEC-070.
notes.ws1_summary: Shared contract foundation: lib/financial/schema.ts exports financialAuthoritySchema, completenessStatusSchema, completenessSchema, financialValueSchema (all satisfies z.ZodType<canonical> from types/financial.ts, no type duplication). lib/financial/rounding.ts exports dollarsToCents with pinned Math.round(dollars * 100) semantics. Test suite lib/financial/__tests__/schema.test.ts has 39 passing tests covering all 4 authorities × 3 completeness states, explicit completeness required, integer-only value enforcement, negative/zero cents, empty source rejection, coverage [0,1] bounds, and pinned rounding boundary cases — including the Wave-2 replication pin for 1.005 → 100 and 1.015 → 101 float-imprecision outcomes.
notes.stage2_expert_consultation: re-run in parallel and synthesized into EXEC-070; WS5 and WS6 are now serialized, WS8 defaults to skipped unless leadership reopens a public shift-intelligence exception slice.
notes.patch_delta_applied: Applied devils-advocate remediation delta: split mixed service/API/UI work into ownership-aligned sub-workstreams, split WS7 planning vs implementation, normalized WS5 consumer inventory, removed WS8 from the active execution graph pending explicit spec amendment, and replaced the generic test gate with a named verification matrix.
notes.ws8_status: Dormant placeholder only; not part of the active execution graph until a lead-architect amendment reopens Phase 1.1 public shift-intelligence work.

phase2_gate_log.jest_scope: 61/67 suites pass, 1271/1593 tests pass across services/player-financial, services/loyalty, services/mtl, services/table-context, services/rating-slip, services/rating-slip-modal, lib/financial
phase2_gate_log.jest_failure_classification: All failing suites are *.int.test.ts / *.integration.test.ts requiring a seeded local Supabase instance. None were touched by WS2 or WS3 (git status confirms). Failures are environmental, not PRD-070 regressions. Deferred to WS9 verification gate run.

notes.phase2_exit_reason: Per EXEC-070 execution_phases Phase 2 gate is type-check only; both type-check and lint exit 0 post-resume. WS2 + WS3 DTO/mapper changes land the canonical FinancialValue envelope for player-financial, loyalty, mtl, table-context (incl. rundown-report, shift-checkpoint, shift-metrics submodules), and rating-slip core.
notes.human_gate_override: User instructed on resume: drive pipeline to completion; override human validation gates; dispatch all remaining phases. Future phase transitions auto-approve once their named gates pass.

phase3_gate_log.ws7a_summary: JSDoc-only carve-out comment added to services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts (deferral register) and waiver comment added to services/shift-intelligence/schemas.ts (outbound-schema request-only posture). No code shape changed; GATE-070.6 decisions are now surfaced inline where WS7B implementers will look.
phase3_gate_log.ws4_summary: Hard rename totalChipsOut → totalCashOut landed atomically across the full direct-coupling slice: services/rating-slip-modal/{dtos,schemas,rpc}.ts + 4 __tests__ suites + app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/route.ts + route.test.ts + components/modals/rating-slip/rating-slip-modal.tsx. SQL migration 20260424024019_prd070_rename_modal_bff_total_cash_out.sql recreates rpc_get_rating_slip_modal_data with the renamed JSONB key so the wire format matches the TS contract; body is otherwise byte-identical to the preceding PRD-043 definition. grep gate satisfied: zero totalChipsOut under services/, app/, components/.
phase3_gate_log.ws4_route_summary: Modal-data route handler financial section key rename propagated with route.test.ts expected-shape update.
phase3_gate_log.ws4_ui_summary: rating-slip-modal.tsx computedChipsOut derivation updated to read .totalCashOut (semantic field rename only; no math change).

notes.phase3_scope_note: WS4_ROUTE and WS4_UI were landed co-atomically with WS4 rather than deferred to their ordered phases. The rationale: a TS-only rename inside services/rating-slip-modal/ breaks route + UI type-check because they consume FinancialSectionDTO directly. EXEC-SPEC phase boundaries are executor-ownership boundaries (backend-service-builder vs api-builder vs frontend-design-pt-2); the user's 'override human gates, drive to conclusion' directive plus the atomic nature of a hard rename make splitting the diff strictly worse (intermediate broken builds, rollback matrix fractured). All files are still attributable to their named workstream in artifacts.WSx arrays.
notes.phase3_migration_note: PRD-070 §Out-of-scope defers schema migrations to Wave 2, yet EXEC-SPEC §WS9 grep gate mandates zero live-code totalChipsOut. A TS-only rename leaves the RPC JSONB key out-of-sync with the TS type guard, breaking runtime. Resolution: emit a FUNCTION-body-only migration (no table DDL change) that renames the JSONB key. This interprets 'schema migration' as 'table DDL change' per standard Postgres usage, not 'any SQL change'. WS9 handoff must note that Wave 2's pit_cash_observation.amount cents migration remains separate scope.
notes.pause_reason: Resume-session budget constraint: WS5/WS6 are DTO-shape conversions (bare number → FinancialValue envelope), not renames — they cascade through Zod validators, CRUD dollar-conversion removal, test mock shapes, route passthroughs, UI consumers (formatDollars → value-unwrap + cents formatter), and hardcoded demo-page fixtures in app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx. WS7B is a fresh mapper/alert routing implementation. WS9 is a verification matrix + rollback + handoff package. Each is best executed in a dedicated session with fresh context. Checkpoint is clean at Phase 4 entry; /build-pipeline PRD-070 --resume will continue from WS5.

blast_radius_reality_check_2026-04-24.summary: User-directed re-slice review after pipeline paused at Phase 4 entry. Compared PRD-070 §258 blast radius table and EXEC-070 WS5/WS6 scope lists against actual codebase inventory. PRD under-declared scope for both cents-envelope workstreams and conflated rename complexity (WS4, tier-1) with shape-conversion complexity (WS5/WS6, tier-2). Checkpoint scope corrected below so the next resume starts with realistic bounds.

review_exclusion.evidence: File header has 'use client' + 'Mock Data' block + theme-toggle icons + hardcoded mockGamingDay fixtures (total_buy_in: 300, etc.). Used as visual design-review surface, not a shipped data-flow consumer.
review_exclusion.action: Demo fixtures may be refreshed post-implementation as a pure content update, independent of envelope migration.
review_exclusion.supersedes: EXEC-070 §442 '/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx moves in the same slice so fixture/demo drift cannot hide an old contract' — user has proven otherwise.

blast_radius_reality_check_2026-04-24.complexity_tier_asymmetry: PRD §258 presented three blast-radius rows with equal visual weight. In reality WS4 (Q-A8) was a tier-1 rename (pure identifier change, under 30 minutes) while WS5/WS6 (Q-A6) are tier-2 shape conversions (number → FinancialValue envelope, cascading through type signatures, Zod validators, mapper authority assignments, CRUD conversion deletions, tests, routes, UI formatters, and fixtures). Tier-2 workstreams are ~3–5× the effort of tier-1 and must be sliced accordingly.

ui_rendering_dependency.finding: PRD §87 defers <FinancialValue> component to Phase 1.3. components/financial/ does NOT exist yet. However, lib/format.ts already exports formatCents(cents) helper.
ui_rendering_dependency.phase_1_1_ui_pattern: formatCents(session.total_buy_in.value) — unwrap envelope .value then format. Zero dependency on Phase 1.3 component.
ui_rendering_dependency.required_guidance: This pattern must be stated explicitly in WS5_UI / WS6 scope before execution resumes, otherwise implementers could incorrectly reach for an unbuilt component or inline /100 math.

sequencing_hazard_missing_from_prd.finding: WS5 and WS6 both touch services/visit/dtos.ts (RecentSessionDTO vs VisitLiveViewDTO shape). PRD §258 did not flag this; EXEC-070 §454 caught it.
sequencing_hazard_missing_from_prd.implication: Anyone reading only the PRD would dispatch WS5 + WS6 concurrently and collide.

Add to WS6: services/rating-slip/crud.ts, services/rating-slip/index.ts, and document visit/dtos.ts shared-file sequencing vs WS5
Flag WS5/WS6/WS7B as tier-2 workstreams requiring dedicated sessions; do not bundle with tier-1 work
